module.name: agent
module.purpose: Agent crate namespace for session runtime orchestration, model backend abstractions, and the implemented task execution runtime. lib.rs is re-export wiring that re-exports SessionState and TaskState; the session child module owns Session and SessionState, the model child module owns the backend abstraction and Deepseek backend, and the task child module owns the Task runtime, shared TaskState state, step, and execution types. The session/task runtimes were refactored so worker, routing, and step logic now hangs off the shared state and takes self: Session and Task are thin (Session::new spawns Arc::clone(&context).spawn_client_worker()/spawn_host_worker(), Task::new spawns TaskState::event_loop and raise() calls Arc::clone(&self.context).run_step(step)), while the worker/routing/step behavior is implemented as private impl SessionState and impl TaskState blocks in session.rs and task.rs (spawn/run/create/route on SessionState; event_loop/run_step plus the model-step methods on TaskState); the session_state.rs and task_state.rs files hold only the plain SessionState and TaskState data structs. The agent's between-session network channels carry the SessionMessage envelope (timestamp + source_name + wrapped SessionEvent): Session::new(name) stores the session name in a module static and exposes a static package_message(SessionEvent) -> SessionMessage that the session and its tasks use to stamp outbound events at the network boundary, while internal per-task control channels stay SessionEvent. The crate also ships an executable entry point: main.rs is the marix-agent binary that loads the global Config (exiting on error), exits when config.agent.enabled is false, constructs marix_agent::Session::new(config.name) (which spawns the client and host accept workers), and parks the main thread forever; agent/Cargo.toml declares the marix-agent [[bin]] target alongside the existing [lib].

childModules.name: model
childModules.purpose: Owns agent model backend traits, request/response data, backend errors, and the implemented Deepseek streaming backend. ModelRequest carries the model-facing task Step and derives only Debug/Clone now that Step is no longer Eq. ModelBackendImpl exposes only send, bridged to ModelBackend by a blanket impl; DeepseekBackend loads its config and issues streaming Deepseek chat-completions requests, and ModelBackendError converts io, serde_json, and reqwest errors.

childModules.name: session
childModules.purpose: Owns Session startup plus the SessionState task/event routing behavior behind the agent session namespace. session.rs defines Session (context: Arc<SessionState>, client/host worker handles), whose new(name) stores the name in a SOURCE_NAME static and spawns workers via Arc::clone(&context).spawn_client_worker()/spawn_host_worker(), plus the static package_message(SessionEvent) -> SessionMessage; below a // -- Private -- // marker session.rs also holds the private impl SessionState behavior block (spawn_client_worker/spawn_host_worker returning JoinHandles, run_client_worker/run_host_worker, create_task, route_session_event/route_task_event, parse_address) whose methods take self/Arc<Self>, guard the shared client/host SessionMessage endpoints with std::sync::Mutex, accept_channel::<SessionMessage> and unwrap message.event, and package the CreateFailed event via Session::package_message before try_send. session_state.rs (renamed from session_context.rs) is the plain SessionState data struct with only pub fn new().

childModules.name: task
childModules.purpose: Owns the implemented task runtime. task.rs defines Task (context: Arc<TaskState>, task_tx, worker), whose new spawns TaskState::event_loop, plus sender() and raise() (Arc::clone(&self.context).run_step(step)); below a // -- Private -- // marker task.rs holds the private impl Task { build_model_backend } helper and the private impl TaskState behavior block (event_loop/run_step plus the private initial_step/execute_model_step/emit_status/send_event) whose methods take self/&self and stream task status plus forwarded host execution events by synchronously locking client_tx (std::sync::Mutex), packaging each SessionEvent into a SessionMessage via Session::package_message, and calling try_send. task_state.rs (renamed from task_context.rs) is the plain TaskState data struct (signature, std::sync::Mutex-guarded model backend, client/host SharedNetSender<SessionMessage> network senders, execution and step WorkQueues) with only pub fn new(). step.rs defines the Step record (signature: StepSignature, status: StepStatus, update_count: Arc<AtomicUsize>) built on the protocol StepSignature/StepStatus contracts, and execution.rs defines the Execution record; run_step keys the step WorkQueue by step.signature.step_no and initial_step builds a Step whose StepSignature.kind is StepKind::Model(ModelStepKind::Initial) with StepStatus::Prepare; the local task_tx/task_rx control channel stays SessionEvent; mod.rs is namespace and re-export wiring only.
